_meta.description: Append-only expansion for automatic record creation rules covering monitoring, queue behavior, single vs multiple queue tracking, unknown senders, entitlements, wait-after-resolution, subject-change handling, multiple mailbox scenarios, customer/agent scenarios, reopen resolved case, and FAQs.

mcq.q: Which activity types are supported out of the box for automatic record creation and update rules in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: Email only
Email, social activity, task, phone call, appointment, service activity, and custom activity
Email, phone call, task, and knowledge article only
Email and custom activity only
mcq.explanation: Dynamics 365 Customer Service supports automatic record creation from Email, Social activity, Task, Phone call, Appointment, Service activity, and Custom activity by default.
wrongExplanations: Email is common, but it is not the only supported source activity.
Knowledge article is not one of the supported source activities for ARC rules.
Custom activity is supported, but the supported list is broader than just email and custom activity.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: Which statement about queues in automatic record creation and update rules is correct?
options: A queue is optional for email and mandatory for custom activities
A queue is mandatory for email source type and optional for other source types including custom activities
A queue is mandatory for all source activity types
Queues are no longer used by modern ARC rules
mcq.explanation: For email source type, specifying a queue is mandatory. For all other source types, including custom activities, the queue is optional.
wrongExplanations: This reverses the documented requirement.
Microsoft explicitly states the queue requirement is mandatory for email but optional for other source types.
Modern ARC rules still use queues.

msRef.label: Set up rules to automatically create or update records

mcq.q: A queue is already used in a single-queue ARC rule. What must happen before that same queue can be used in a multiple-queues tracking rule?
options: Nothing; a queue can be used in both rule types at the same time
The existing rule must be deactivated first
The queue must be converted into a shared mailbox
The queue must be removed from server-side synchronization
mcq.explanation: A queue can't be used as part of both a single-queue tracking rule and a multiple-queues tracking rule at the same time. You must deactivate the existing rule first.
wrongExplanations: Microsoft explicitly says the same queue cannot be used in both tracking types simultaneously.
Mailbox type conversion is not the documented requirement.
Server-side sync removal is not the documented requirement.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: Can a single-queue ARC rule be converted into a multiple-queues ARC rule, or vice versa?
options: Yes, by editing the Queue scope after activation
Yes, but only if no rule items exist
No; you must create a new rule
Yes, through the activity monitor
mcq.explanation: You can't convert a single-queue ARC rule into a multiple-queues ARC rule, or vice versa. Microsoft says you need to create a new rule.
wrongExplanations: Queue scope is not a supported conversion path for this.
The limitation is not dependent on whether rule items exist.
Activity monitor does not convert rule scope.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: What is true about the activity monitor for automatic record creation rules?
options: It works in on-premises and online environments equally
It tracks only what happens after Power Automate runs
It works in online environments and tracks rules until they are evaluated in Customer Service, before the rule runs in Power Automate
It is available only for legacy workflow-based ARC rules
mcq.explanation: The activity monitor works in online environments only and tracks ARC rules until they are evaluated in Customer Service, before the rule runs in Power Automate.
wrongExplanations: Microsoft says activity monitor works in online environments only.
It does not track only post-Power-Automate execution; it tracks up to the pre-flow evaluation point.
It is not limited to legacy workflow-based rules.

msRef.label: Use activity monitor to review and track rules

mcq.q: Where can administrators view the activity monitor summary for the last seven days?
options: Power Platform admin center > Environment history
Copilot Service admin center > Customer support > Overview > Case settings
Customer Service workspace > Queue diagnostics
Power Automate > Flow analytics
mcq.explanation: In Copilot Service admin center, Customer support > Overview > Case settings displays the number of activity monitor events that ran in the past week along with their status.
wrongExplanations: Environment history is not where ARC activity monitor summaries are surfaced.
Queue diagnostics are not the documented location for ARC activity monitor summary.
Flow analytics is for Power Automate runs, not the ARC activity monitor summary.

msRef.label: Use activity monitor to review and track rules

mcq.q: In advanced ARC settings, what does 'Allow emails from unknown senders' control?
options: Whether the queue accepts internet mail
Whether records can be created when the sender address isn't present on any contact or account
Whether replies from inactive contacts reopen a resolved case
Whether the rule can use multiple queues
mcq.explanation: Allow emails from unknown senders determines whether records are created when email messages arrive from senders whose email addresses aren't present on any contact or account records.
wrongExplanations: Queue mail acceptance is a different concern.
Resolved-case reopen behavior is a separate scenario.
Multiple queue tracking is unrelated to unknown sender handling.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: If administrators choose to manage unknown senders by mapping in Power Automate manually, what must they ensure?
options: That duplicate detection is turned off
That they create a mapping in Power Automate for the Customer field
That all queues are public queues
That the rule owner is always a team
mcq.explanation: Microsoft warns that if you select manual mapping for unknown senders, you must create a mapping in Power Automate for the customer field.
wrongExplanations: Duplicate detection is not the documented requirement here.
Queue privacy is unrelated to the customer-field mapping requirement.
Rule ownership is unrelated to this requirement.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: When 'Require a valid entitlement on the connected case' is set to Yes, under which condition can a case still be created for a contact sender with a parent account?
options: Only if the contact itself owns the entitlement record
If the parent account has an active entitlement and the contact is listed on it, or the entitlement contact list is empty
Only if the email subject contains the case number
Only if the queue owner is a user, not a team
mcq.explanation: If the sender is a contact with a parent account, a record can be created if the contact's parent account has a valid entitlement and the contact is listed in the entitlement's Contacts section, or the Contacts section is empty.
wrongExplanations: The entitlement can belong to the parent account rather than the contact itself.
Case number in the subject is unrelated to entitlement validation.
Queue owner type is unrelated to entitlement validation.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: What happens if 'Wait for a specific amount of time after the connected case has been resolved' is set to No and a related case already exists?
options: The existing resolved case is reopened automatically
The system doesn't create a case
The system always creates a new case immediately
The email is deleted from the queue
mcq.explanation: Microsoft states that if this setting is set to No, the system doesn't create a case if a related case exists.
wrongExplanations: Resolved cases are not reopened automatically in this scenario.
Immediate new-case creation is not the documented behavior when this is No and a related case exists.
The email is not deleted from the queue because of this setting.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: What is the documented way to create a case without any time lapse after the connected case has been resolved?
options: Set Wait for a specific amount of time to No
Set Wait for a specific amount of time to Yes and leave the duration blank
Set the rule item order so the new-case item runs first
Turn off queue item creation for synchronized mailboxes
mcq.explanation: If you want a case to be created without any time lapse, Microsoft says to set the Wait toggle to Yes and leave the time duration unselected.
wrongExplanations: Setting it to No does not produce that documented behavior.
Rule item order does not replace this setting.
Queue item creation settings are unrelated.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: How are multiple ARC rule items evaluated within a rule?
options: All matching rule items run in parallel
Rules are evaluated alphabetically by condition name
Rules run in listed order, and once a rule item matches and creates the case, the remaining rule items aren't evaluated
Only the newest rule item is evaluated
mcq.explanation: ARC rule items are run in the order listed. If the incoming activity matches the condition in one rule item and the system creates the case, the remaining rule items aren't evaluated.
wrongExplanations: They are not evaluated in parallel.
Alphabetical evaluation is not the documented behavior.
Only the newest rule item is not the evaluation model.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

mcq.q: To create a new case when an email subject changes, what table should the custom flow monitor according to Microsoft's pattern?
options: Emails
Cases
Activity monitors
Queues
mcq.explanation: Microsoft's subject-change pattern uses a custom flow triggered when a row is added to the Activity monitors table with a skipped state.
wrongExplanations: The documented trigger pattern is not a direct Email-table trigger.
Cases are not the documented trigger source for this pattern.
Queues are not the documented trigger source for this pattern.

msRef.label: Create a new case when email subject changes

mcq.q: Which activity monitor state is used in Microsoft's custom-flow pattern for creating a new case when the email subject changes?
options: Ready
Skipped
Failed
Completed
mcq.explanation: The documented custom flow pattern is triggered when case creation is skipped for an email and there's an activity monitor entry in the Skipped state.
wrongExplanations: Ready is not the documented state for this pattern.
Failed is not the documented state for this pattern.
Completed is not the documented state for this pattern.

msRef.label: Create a new case when email subject changes

mcq.q: Which of the following is a documented reason that can appear for a skipped activity monitor entry in the email-subject-change scenario?
options: The queue owner is inactive
An active case is already connected with this record
The mailbox isn't approved
The email contains an unsupported attachment
mcq.explanation: Microsoft lists reasons such as 'An active case is already connected with this record' as one of the skip reasons you can filter on in the subject-change custom flow pattern.
wrongExplanations: Inactive queue owner is not one of the documented reasons in this pattern.
Mailbox approval is not one of the listed skip reasons here.
Unsupported attachment is not one of the listed skip reasons here.

msRef.label: Create a new case when email subject changes

mcq.q: To help representatives identify all queues from which a case originated when one email was sent to multiple mailboxes, which table does Microsoft tell you to expose?
options: Queue Item
Originating Queue Mapping
Mailbox
Activity monitors
mcq.explanation: Microsoft's multiple-mailboxes guidance tells you to create a view on the Originating Queue Mapping table and add a subgrid for it to the case form.
wrongExplanations: Queue Item is related but not the table Microsoft tells you to expose for this feature.
Mailbox is not the documented table to surface for this purpose.
Activity monitors are for monitoring rule processing, not for surfacing originating queues.

msRef.label: Create multiple cases from an email sent to multiple mailboxes

mcq.q: In the customer scenario, what happens when a customer forwards a service representative's reply email from Inbox to a new queue?
options: ARC always skips case creation because the mail was forwarded
ARC creates a new case for the new queue, and the email is tagged to the existing queue-originated cases and the new case
ARC reopens the original case only
ARC creates cases for every queue in the tenant
mcq.explanation: Microsoft documents that forwarding a representative's reply mail from the inbox to a new queue creates a new case for that new queue, and the email is tagged to the earlier queue-originated cases as well as the new case.
wrongExplanations: Forwarding to a new queue is specifically documented as a new-case scenario, not always skipped.
The documented behavior is not limited to reopening the original case.
ARC does not create cases for every queue in the tenant.

msRef.label: How the automatic record creation rule works in a customer scenario

mcq.q: When a service representative sends a new email from the case timeline, under what condition is the Related attribute set to the case?
options: Always, by default
Only if the OrgDBOrg setting AddParentEntityToRelatedOnNewActivityCreate is enabled
Only if the queue has ARC disabled
Only if the email is created from reply-all
mcq.explanation: In the agent scenario, the Related attribute is set to the case only if the administrator has enabled the OrgDBOrg setting AddParentEntityToRelatedOnNewActivityCreate.
wrongExplanations: Microsoft says this does not happen automatically in all cases.
ARC enablement on the queue is unrelated to this specific behavior.
Microsoft explicitly says this applies to new emails from the timeline, not reply/reply all scenarios.

msRef.label: How the automatic record creation rule works in a customer service representative scenario

mcq.q: In Microsoft's reopen-resolved-case pattern, where should the reopen rule item appear relative to the create-new-case rule item?
options: Below it, so the new case logic runs first
Above it, so reopen is evaluated first
Order does not matter if both use Power Automate
In a separate rule only
mcq.explanation: Microsoft explicitly says the reopen resolved case rule item should appear above the create new case rule item so it is evaluated first.
wrongExplanations: That would let new-case creation run before the reopen logic.
Order still matters.
Microsoft's documented pattern is about the ordering of rule items.

msRef.label: Reopen a resolved case in Dynamics 365 Customer Service

mcq.q: Which advanced ARC setting must be configured for Microsoft's reopen-resolved-case pattern, and how should it be set?
options: Allow emails from unknown senders = Yes
Require a valid entitlement = No
Wait for a specific amount of time after the connected case has been resolved = Yes, with no duration required
Automatically reply to email = Yes
mcq.explanation: Microsoft says the Wait for a specific amount of time after the connected case has been resolved setting should be set to Yes for the reopen-resolved-case pattern, and there is no need to provide a time value.
wrongExplanations: Unknown sender handling is unrelated to the reopen-resolved-case pattern.
Entitlement requirement is unrelated to the reopen-resolved-case pattern.
Automatic reply is unrelated to the reopen-resolved-case pattern.

msRef.label: Reopen a resolved case in Dynamics 365 Customer Service

tf.q: True or False: The activity monitor can be used in on-premises environments for automatic record creation rules.
tf.explanation: False. Microsoft says the activity monitor works in online environments only.

msRef.label: Use activity monitor to review and track rules

tf.q: True or False: If a rule remains in Draft status, it can still process incoming emails as long as the queue is configured correctly.
tf.explanation: False. The rule remains inactive while in Draft status and won't process incoming activities until activated.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

tf.q: True or False: If a rule owner is a team, the owner of a case created through the automatic record creation rule is the team's administrator user.
tf.explanation: True. Microsoft documents that when a case is created through an ARC rule and the rule owner is a team, the owner of the created case is the team's administrator user.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

tf.q: True or False: If the reason is empty and the state says Ready for workflow/Power Automate in activity monitor, Microsoft says to check system jobs for modern rules.
tf.explanation: False. Microsoft says to check system jobs if it's a legacy rule, or check flow runs if it's a modern rule.

msRef.label: FAQ about automatic record creation

dragdrop.instruction: Match each ARC setting or artifact to its correct meaning.
items: Require a valid entitlement on the connected case
Wait for a specific amount of time after the connected case has been resolved
zones: Mandatory for email source type and defines where the incoming activity is processed
Lets the rule create records for senders not found on contact or account
Allows case creation only when entitlement validation is satisfied
Controls whether and when a new case can be created after a related case was resolved
Tracks ARC processing health before Power Automate runs

correctMapping.Queue to monitor: Mandatory for email source type and defines where the incoming activity is processed
correctMapping.Allow emails from unknown senders: Lets the rule create records for senders not found on contact or account
correctMapping.Require a valid entitlement on the conne: Allows case creation only when entitlement validation is satisfied
correctMapping.Wait for a specific amount of time after: Controls whether and when a new case can be created after a related case was resolved
correctMapping.Activity monitor: Tracks ARC processing health before Power Automate runs

dragdrop.explanation: These settings control source queue behavior, unknown sender handling, entitlement checks, timing after case resolution, and pre-flow monitoring.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

dragdrop.instruction: Put the modern ARC setup flow in the correct order.
items: Create the rule and enter basic details
Save so the condition/action area is enabled
Add at least one condition in the condition builder
Configure the record to create and open Power Automate
dragdrop.explanation: Microsoft's modern ARC setup starts with creating the rule and basic details, saving it, adding at least one condition, configuring the target record and Power Automate logic, then activating the rule.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

dragdrop.instruction: Match each scenario to the documented ARC outcome.
items: Customer forwards representative reply to same queue from Inbox
Customer forwards representative reply to a new queue from Inbox
Service representative creates a new email from case timeline
zones: A new case is created for the new queue
Related is set to the case only if a specific OrgDBOrg setting is enabled
The reopen rule item must be evaluated before the create-new-case item
Uses a Skipped activity monitor entry to trigger custom logic

correctMapping.Customer forwards representative reply t: A new case is created for the new queue
correctMapping.Service representative creates a new ema: Related is set to the case only if a specific OrgDBOrg setting is enabled
correctMapping.Reopen-resolved-case pattern: The reopen rule item must be evaluated before the create-new-case item
correctMapping.Subject-change custom flow: Uses a Skipped activity monitor entry to trigger custom logic

dragdrop.explanation: These scenario outcomes come directly from Microsoft's customer scenario, agent scenario, subject-change, and reopen-resolved-case guidance.

msRef.label: How the automatic record creation rule works in a customer scenario

casestudy.title: Northwind Email-to-Case Expansion
casestudy.scenario: Northwind is expanding its email-to-case setup. The support organization uses several monitored queues, wants to allow some unknown senders, and needs rules to behave differently when related cases are active or recently resolved. The architects also want visibility into skipped and failed processing without jumping straight into Power Automate runs.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

questions.q: Northwind is using email as the source activity type. What is mandatory before the rule can be activated?
options: At least one queue must be selected for the rule
The rule owner must be a team
The subject-change flow must exist
All mailboxes must be private queues
questions.explanation: For email activity, Microsoft says you must select a queue to be able to activate the rule.
wrongExplanations: Rule ownership by a team is not mandatory for activation.
Subject-change customization is optional and scenario-specific.
Queue privacy is unrelated.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

questions.q: Northwind wants insight into rule health before Power Automate logic starts. Which feature fits that requirement?
options: Activity monitor
Flow analytics only
Audit history on the case
Queue item diagnostics
questions.explanation: The activity monitor tracks ARC processing until it is evaluated in Customer Service and before the rule runs in Power Automate.
wrongExplanations: Flow analytics focuses on flow runs rather than the pre-flow ARC evaluation stage.
Case audit history is not the rule-health monitor.
Queue diagnostics are not the documented ARC monitor for this requirement.

msRef.label: Use activity monitor to review and track rules

questions.q: Northwind wants a case created even if no matching contact or account exists for the sender. Which advanced setting matters first?
options: Allow emails from unknown senders
Automatically reply to email
Wait for a specific amount of time after the connected case has been resolved
Track multiple queues
questions.explanation: Allow emails from unknown senders is the setting that controls whether records can be created when the sender address isn't present on any contact or account.
wrongExplanations: Automatic reply is unrelated to whether a case is created for an unknown sender.
The wait-after-resolution setting addresses related-case timing, not unknown sender creation.
Queue scope is unrelated to unknown sender handling.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

questions.q: Northwind wants no delay before a new case can be created after a connected case is resolved. Which configuration matches Microsoft's guidance?
options: Set Wait after resolution to No
Set Wait after resolution to Yes and leave the duration blank
Set the duration to zero minutes
Use multiple queue tracking
questions.explanation: Microsoft says to set the wait toggle to Yes and leave the duration blank if you want a case to be created without any time lapse.
wrongExplanations: Setting it to No does not produce that documented behavior.
Zero minutes is not the documented instruction.
Queue tracking mode is unrelated.

msRef.label: Automatically create or update records

questions.q: Northwind sees a skipped activity monitor entry because an active case is already connected to the record, and wants custom subject-change logic. What pattern should they use?
options: Trigger a custom flow from the Activity monitors table on skipped entries
Create a business rule on the Email table
Use a queue routing rule to split by subject
Replace ARC with unified routing
questions.explanation: Microsoft's subject-change guidance uses a custom flow triggered by added Activity monitor rows in the Skipped state, then checks subject change and calls the ARC child flow.
wrongExplanations: A business rule on Email is not the documented pattern.
Queue routing rules are not the documented pattern here.
Unified routing does not replace this ARC customization pattern.

msRef.label: Create a new case when email subject changes

casestudy.title: Contoso Multi-Queue and Reopen Design
casestudy.scenario: Contoso monitors several support mailboxes. One inbound email can reach multiple queues, and the company wants representatives to see all originating queues on the case. They also want emails on recently resolved issues to reopen the old case under controlled conditions instead of always creating a new one.

msRef.label: Create multiple cases from an email sent to multiple mailboxes

questions.q: What table should Contoso expose on the case form to show all originating queues for a multi-mailbox email scenario?
options: Originating Queue Mapping
Mailbox
Activity monitors
Queue Item
questions.explanation: Microsoft tells administrators to create a view on the Originating Queue Mapping table and add a subgrid for it to the case form.
wrongExplanations: Mailbox is not the documented table to expose for this purpose.
Activity monitors are for monitoring rule health, not originating queue visibility.
Queue Item is related but not the documented table for this scenario.

msRef.label: Create multiple cases from an email sent to multiple mailboxes

questions.q: If a customer forwards a representative's reply email from Inbox to a new queue, what does Microsoft document?
options: ARC skips case creation because the mail was forwarded
A new case is created for the new queue
The original case is reopened automatically
No email tagging occurs on the earlier cases
questions.explanation: In the customer scenario, Microsoft says ARC creates a new case for the new queue when the forwarded email is sent from the inbox to a new queue.
wrongExplanations: That is the same-queue forwarded scenario, not the new-queue scenario.
Automatic reopen is not the documented outcome here.
Microsoft says the email is tagged to earlier queue-originated cases as well.

msRef.label: How the automatic record creation rule works in a customer scenario

questions.q: Where should the reopen-resolved-case rule item be placed relative to the create-new-case rule item?
options: After it
Before it
In a different solution only
Order is irrelevant
questions.explanation: Microsoft says the reopen rule item must appear above the create-new-case rule item so it is evaluated first.
wrongExplanations: That would let create-new-case logic run too early.
This is about rule item order, not solution packaging.
Microsoft explicitly says order matters.

msRef.label: Reopen a resolved case in Dynamics 365 Customer Service

questions.q: What should the advanced 'Wait after connected case has been resolved' setting be for Microsoft's reopen pattern?
options: No
Yes, with no duration required
Yes, exactly one hour
Only if unknown senders are disabled
questions.explanation: The reopen-resolved-case article says to set the wait-after-resolution setting to Yes, with no need to provide a time value.
wrongExplanations: No is not the documented setting for the reopen pattern.
One hour is not the documented requirement.
Unknown sender handling is unrelated.

msRef.label: Reopen a resolved case in Dynamics 365 Customer Service

questions.q: Which monitoring artifact is useful when subject-change custom logic depends on skipped ARC processing reasons?
options: Activity monitor entries
Case audit history
App insights traces
Security role audit log
questions.explanation: Subject-change customization is driven from Activity monitor entries in the Skipped state, where the reason can be inspected.
wrongExplanations: Case audit history is not the documented trigger source.
App insights traces are not the documented trigger source.
Security role audit log is unrelated.

msRef.label: Create a new case when email subject changes
